decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the hot spot. I will increase the spreader conductivity by a factor of 1.5 (from 500 to 750 W/(m*K)), which is within the allowed 2.0x step ratio and bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the local thermal gradient and lowering the peak chip temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
Ensure the new conductivity value remains physically realistic for the selected material class.
